questions.statement: A 270-m-long train takes 24 seconds to cross a pole. If it takes 1 minute to cross a tunnel, then the length (in m) of the tunnel is:
A 338-m-long train is travelling at 60 km/h. It crosses a man, travelling in the same direction, in 39 seconds. What is the speed (in m/sec) of the man?
<p>The ratio of the speeds of two trains is 5 : 8. If the second train runs 400 km in 4 hours, then the speed of the first train (in km/h) is:&nbsp;</p>
A 725 m long train passes through a 235 m long tunnel in 48 sec. Find the speed of the train.
A train travelling at a speed of 36 km/h crosses a pole in 24 sec. What is the length of the train?
